message:
test(runtime): MULTI/EXEC/WATCH linearizability harness (br-frankenredis-b6ka)

Adds crates/fr-runtime/tests/multi_exec_linearizability.rs — 11 tests
exercising the two transaction contracts against the single-thread
eventloop. Client interleaving is simulated via Runtime::swap_session
so two persistent ClientSessions A and B can alternate commands in an
arbitrary order.

Spec-derived matrix (M1..M5):
  M1  Plain MULTI/EXEC applies the batch and returns the per-command
      reply array.
  M2  WATCH without a concurrent write → EXEC applies.
  M3  WATCH k, B writes k between WATCH and EXEC → EXEC returns RESP2
      null-array (Array(None)); k holds B's value.
  M4  WATCH k1 k2, B modifies only k2 → whole batch aborts; neither k1
      nor k2 takes A's intended value.
  M5  Aborted EXEC does not poison the next MULTI/EXEC in the same
      session.

Metamorphic relations:
  MR-WATCH-MONOTONIC   any N∈{1,3,10,50} intervening writes produce
                       the same ABORT verdict — the flag is binary.
  MR-WATCH-IDEMPOTENT  WATCH k; WATCH k behaves identically to WATCH k.
  MR-UNWATCH-CLEARS    UNWATCH drops version pins; a subsequent WATCH
                       captures fresh state so EXEC applies.
  MR-DISCARD-CLEARS    DISCARD between MULTI and EXEC wipes the queue
                       and releases WATCHes; EXEC after DISCARD returns
                       the canonical "EXEC without MULTI" error.

Plus regression guards for:
  - EXEC outside MULTI → canonical error
  - WATCH inside MULTI → canonical error

Pins one important RESP convention: EXEC on an aborted transaction
returns the RESP2 null-array frame (*-1\r\n), decoded as Array(None),
NOT the null-bulk (BulkString(None)). The `exec_aborted()` helper
and its docstring call out the distinction.

No implementation change; test-only addition. 11 new tests pass in
cargo test -p fr-runtime --test multi_exec_linearizability.

Co-Authored-By: cc-redis <noreply@anthropic.com>
